texto: But as in the degrees of sickness thou art to submit to God, so in the kind of it (supposing equal degrees) thou art to be altogether indifferent whether God call thee by a consumption or an asthma, by a dropsy or palsy, by a fever in thy humours, or a fever in your spirits; because all such nicety of choice is nothing but a colour to a legitimate impatience, and to make an excuse to murmur privately, and for circumstances, when in the sum of affairs we durst not own impatience.”Jeremy Taylor’s “Holy Dying”, extract from chapter IV.I (The Practice of Patience) para 5.
explicacao: Citação de Jeremey Taylor sobre temas ligados a fe. “But as in the degrees of sickness thou art to submit to God, so in the kind of it (supposing equa…” articula fe, affair, asthma em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como luto e asthma.
